name: SECURITY NATIONAL FINANCIAL CORP
summary: Security National Financial Corporation operates three business segments: life insurance, cemetery/mortuary, and mortgages. Through subsidiaries including Security National Life, First Guaranty, and Kilpatrick Life, it sells funeral plans, annuities, and accident/health insurance in 42 states. Its cemetery/mortuary segment operates 11 mortuaries and 6 cemeteries across Utah, California, and New Mexico. SecurityNational Mortgage originates residential and commercial loans through 85 retail offices in 25 states.

products.name: Funeral Plan Life Insurance
products.description: Small face value life insurance policies up to $30,000 designed to cover death-related costs and expenses.

products.name: Single Premium Deferred Annuities
products.description: Annuity contracts where individuals deposit a lump sum retained until annuitization or cash surrender.

products.name: Flexible Premium Deferred Annuities
products.description: Annuity contracts allowing variable premium payments after initial deposit, with guaranteed rates from 1% to 6.5%.

products.name: Immediate Annuities
products.description: Contracts providing periodic payments over a designated period in exchange for a lump-sum remittance.

products.name: Accident and Health Insurance
products.description: Small blocks of accident and health policies acquired through acquisitions; diver accident insurance discontinued March 2024.

products.name: Residential and Commercial Mortgage Loans
products.description: Mortgage origination and underwriting for new construction, existing homes, and real estate projects through 85 retail offices.

products.name: Pre-Need Funeral and Cemetery Services
products.description: Pre-need selling of funeral, cemetery, mortuary, and cremation services through cemetery and mortuary locations.

partnerships.description: Third-party cemeteries and mortuaries provide endorsement letters and share mailing/lead-generating costs for funeral plan insurance marketing.

origin.chain: SEC.gov PEM → origin.rootz.global extraction → GitHub mirror
